news.title: Investors allege ₹1.6 crore fraud in WTC GIFT City scheme
news.para: CID (Crime) has registered a complaint alleging financial fraud exceeding Rs 1.56 crore in a GIFT City investment scheme. Investors were promised high monthly returns and a buyback guarantee for commercial units, but their funds were allegedly not repaid after payments stopped in September 2022. Post-dated cheques for repayment were dishonoured, prompting an investigation into the alleged fraud.

news.title: Illegal colonies, walls and sheds razed by DTCP in Gurugram
news.para: The Department of Town and Country Planning (DTCP) intensified its campaign against illegal constructions this week, conducting demolition drives in Sadhrana, Bhondsi, and Sector 89. Authorities razed unauthorized colonies and structures, including foundations, boundary walls, and sheds, to curb encroachment and protect buyers from misleading developments. The action aimed to prevent further illegal plotting and sale of land.

news.title: Magistrate’s court to hear ₹140 crore Guruashish cheating case
news.para: A special court in Mumbai transferred a Rs 140 crore cheating and conspiracy case against Guruashish Construction and its directors to a magistrate's court. The CBI found no evidence of public servant involvement, leading the special court to state it lacked jurisdiction to proceed with the matter against private individuals alone.

news.title: Centre extends PMAY-U 2.0 scheme till September 2026; targets housing for one crore urban families
news.para: The Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana-Urban is now extended until September 2026, with the launch of PMAY-U 2.0. This upgrade will benefit an estimated one crore more urban households over the next five years. The scheme is not just about building homes; it's about embracing modern building practices and sustainable living solutions.

news.title: Scheme to incubate eight new cities cannot be implemented: Housing ministry
news.para: The government has decided to abandon its plan for the development of eight new cities, explaining that inadequate planning frameworks rendered the Rs 8,000 crore grant from the 15th Finance Commission unusable. With a keen eye on effective fund management, the ministry has opted to reallocate the budget towards bolstering seven established city economic zones, aiming for more sustainable growth.

news.title: Brookfield India REIT plans ₹4,000 crore institutional placement; Ecoworld SPV to raise ₹1,125 crore from 360 ONE fund
news.para: Brookfield India REIT is preparing to raise ₹4,000 crore through institutional placements. Arliga Ecoworld Business Parks, its subsidiary, is anticipated to fetch ₹1,125 crore from the 360 ONE WAM funds. These funds will not only aid in acquisitions and debt repayments but also enhance capital expenditures to drive future growth initiatives.

news.title: UK's Savills buys Eastdil Secured for $1.1 billion to expand US presence
news.para: Savills is purchasing Eastdil Secured for $1.1 billion, aiming to enhance its influence in the North American arena and capital markets. The acquisition is projected to boost earnings per share by 2027. Nonetheless, Savills faced a drop in share prices, influenced by ongoing uncertainties surrounding Middle Eastern conflicts.

news.title: JK Lakshmi Cement wins Assam limestone block, to acquire 77.96% stake in NECEM Cements for ₹19 crore
news.para: JK Lakshmi Cement has taken a significant step by securing a strategic limestone block in Assam and acquiring NECEM Cements. This decision will not only fortify the company’s supply chain but also expand its geographical reach in the North East region, promising robust growth with ample land and limestone resources for expansion.

news.title: Assotech Realty targets upto ₹400 crore revenue in FY27; Atmosphere Core eyes $400 million by 2030
news.para: Assotech Realty and Atmosphere Core have partnered to develop a hospitality project in Shirdi, Maharashtra. Assotech plans to scale its revenue to ₹350-400 crore by FY27, while Atmosphere Core targets $400 million by 2030, driven by expansion into spiritual and tourism destinations.

news.title: Maharashtra sets up SIT to probe land complaints in Sambhajinagar
news.para: A Special Investigation Team (SIT) has been formed by the state government to probe major land-related complaints in Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar district. Headed by the divisional commissioner, the SIT will investigate serious allegations in land deals and submit a report within three months. Citizens are urged to submit complaints by April 10.

news.title: Gera Developments appoints Rajesh Shirwatkar as chief financial officer
news.para: Gera Developments has appointed Rajesh Yashwant Shirwatkar as its new Chief Financial Officer. In his new role, Shirwatkar will oversee the company's financial strategy, planning, compliance, treasury, and governance. Concurrently, Swapan Mukherjee has been promoted to Group Chief Financial Officer, providing financial oversight for the entire group's growth.

news.title: Prayagraj civic body approves house tax rebate for old buildings
news.para: Prayagraj Municipal Corporation approved a 40% house tax rebate for older buildings. This benefit will be automatic for properties over 20 years old. The decision takes effect from April 2026. Many older city areas will see relief. A One-Time Settlement scheme for tax defaulters is also proposed. The municipal budget was revised downwards.

news.title: Aurum PropTech sells commercial buildings in Navi Mumbai for ₹112 crore
news.para: Aurum PropTech's board greenlit the sale of two Navi Mumbai buildings for ₹112 crore, exceeding valuations. This strategic move, expected by June 2026, will clear the company's debt and fund a pivot towards AI-driven PropTech platforms.  The substantial capital infusion signals a significant shift in their business focus.

news.title: IPO rush among developers reflects better governance, but expansion must be calibrated: Brigade Group's founder
news.para: India's real estate market is booming post-pandemic with premium homes gaining traction. However, mid-income and mass housing remain key. Brigade Group's M.R. Jaishankar highlights the need for government support in affordable housing and developer caution on expansion. The sector's long-term outlook is positive, driven by economic growth and rising incomes.

news.title: Telangana: 412 Velugumatla families get house pattas, Indiramma houses
news.para: Deputy Chief Minister Bhatti Vikramarka announced a model colony for the poor in Velugumatla, Khammam, to be an inspiration for other states. The government distributed house site pattas and sanctioned Indiramma houses to 412 families affected by encroachment demolitions, promising full basic amenities and completion by December 9th.

news.title: Indore: 533 locations to be merged; rates may surge by up to 200%
news.para: Indore's district administration is rationalizing property registration locations by merging 533 existing ones to streamline the process and prevent revenue leakage. Collector's guideline rates for 2026-27 are set to increase by 10% to 200% across approximately 3,000 locations, with significant hikes planned for the city's outskirts.

news.title: Will discuss with CM, deputy CMs rehabilitation of first-floor slum dwellers: Maharashtra minister
news.para: Maharashtra minister Shambhuraj Desai announced that discussions will be held with the Chief Minister and Deputy CMs regarding the demand to include first-floor slum dwellers in SRA rehabilitation schemes. This move aims to address the long-standing issue of residents living on upper floors of slum structures for decades, which currently disqualifies them under existing rules.

news.title: Ludhiana civic body to collect property tax at residents’ door
news.para: Ludhiana Municipal Corporation is launching a first-of-its-kind doorstep tax collection service using handheld POS machines. This initiative aims to recover unpaid municipal dues by visiting properties, calculating taxes on the spot, and accepting various payment modes. The system offers real-time tracking and immediate receipts, targeting defaulters to meet financial goals.

news.title: Split property tax into monthly EMIs: Finance panel to Karnataka government
news.para: Property tax reforms are on the horizon in Bengaluru, with the State Finance Commission proposing monthly installment payments and amnesty schemes to boost collections. The commission also advocates for drone surveys and cross-verification with utility data to identify unregistered properties, aiming to significantly increase revenue for local corporations and ease the burden on taxpayers.

news.title: MCD plans GPS mapping of construction waste sites across Delhi to curb illegal dumping
news.para: The Municipal Corporation of Delhi is implementing GPS mapping for 111 identified construction and demolition waste sites across its 12 zones. This initiative aims to streamline waste collection and disposal, curbing illegal dumping and pollution. Once completed, residents will be able to locate official dumping points online, facilitating proper waste management.
